{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the linear program with free variables \(y\):\n\[\n\min \; c^T x + d^T y \qquad \text{subject to } A_1x + A_2y = b,\quad x \ge 0.\n\]\nIntroduce Lagrange multipliers \(\lambda\) for the equality constraints and \(s\) for the bounds \(x \ge 0\). Write down the optimality conditions for this problem in a form analogous to (14.3). Then, following (14.4) and (14.16), derive the general step equations for a primal-dual interior-point method. Express these equations in augmented-system form analogous to (14.42), and explain why they cannot be reduced further to a form like (14.44) with a symmetric positive definite coefficient matrix."}
